text: @EmiratesNBD_AE @SobhaRealtyDXB 👍 how does a foreigner open a bank account with you?
html: Hello, thanks for reaching us! We’re happy to introduce our new digital way of getting your account opened on the go! if you are a UAE resident You can just download our Emirates NBD mobile app and follow the steps to enjoy your new account with us. You must have AED 5000 salary, valid visa, valid passport and valid Emirates ID For employed individuals any one of the below documents are required: Salary letter/certificate Employment contract Last 6 months Bank statement to open the account through the App. And it must be your first relationship with Emirates NBD. In case you faced any issues while opening the account, please fill the details on the following link and our team will contact you within 48 working hours to assist further, please select Liabilities. ms.spr.ly/6188lRA6p For non-resident Please find the below link for more details and form to fill, and our team will reach you out within 2 working days to assist you. ms.spr.ly/6014vDdfp
text_1:
Hello, thanks for reaching us! We’re happy to introduce our new digital way of getting your account opened on the go! if you are a UAE resident 

You can just download our Emirates NBD mobile app and follow the steps to enjoy your new account with us. You must have AED 5000 salary, valid visa, valid passport and valid Emirates ID For employed individuals any one of the below documents are required:

Salary letter/certificate
Employment contract 
Last 6 months Bank statement

to open the account through the App. And it must be your first relationship with Emirates NBD. In case you faced any issues while opening the account, please fill the details on the following link and our team will contact you within 48 working hours to assist further, please select Liabilities.

ms.spr.ly/6188lRA6p

For non-resident Please find the below link for more details and form to fill, and our team will reach you out within 2 working days to assist you. 

ms.spr.ly/6014vDdfp

A range of financial support and banking services dedicated to frontline workers:
• 10% cashback up to a maximum of 400 dirhams
• Fixed interest rate of 6% for all new employees who transfer their salaries
• Extension of salary transfer period
• Waiver of minimum balance fees for customers whose salaries are not transferred
• Loan fee waivers with priority processing of applications

The offer is valid until June 30, 2026.

Terms and conditions apply.

text:
Dubai South has signed a strategic MoU with Emirates NBD to support SME growth and improve access to corporate banking services across its business community.

The agreement was signed by Saif Al-Ali, Director of Licensing and Free Zone Management at Dubai Aviation City Corporation, and Hamad Mohamed Zayed, Group Head of Business Banking at Emirates NBD, reinforcing a shared focus on creating a stronger support framework for companies operating within Dubai South.

Under the partnership, Emirates NBD will provide streamlined corporate account opening and tailored banking solutions designed to meet the evolving needs of businesses and entrepreneurs in the district.

Nabil Al Kindi, Group CEO of Dubai South, said the collaboration reflects Dubai South’s mandate to support entrepreneurs and SMEs through an integrated ecosystem that enables business growth, operational efficiency, and long-term success.

Hamad Mohamed Zayed, Group Head of Business Banking at Emirates NBD, said the partnership supports Dubai’s wider ambition to double its economy by 2033 and strengthen its position among the world’s top three economic cities.

The MoU marks another step in Dubai South’s broader efforts to enhance its business-friendly environment while aligning with the emirate’s long-term economic and development goals.

Read more at:https://t.co/K32OdV1Zke

post:
SMEs starved for funding finally get a lifeline.

Dubai South signs a Memorandum of Understanding with Emirates NBD to enhance financial access for SMEs in its business zone.

This initiative aims to support the growth of small firms amid the UAE's efforts to diversify its economy beyond oil.

Easier loans could stimulate job creation and investments; the zone is looking to attract more startups as competitors like Qatar expand their free zones.
